title: Executing the SFT Step
concept: Running LoRA fine-tuning epochs on the instruction-code dataset using Cross-Entropy loss.

exercises.prompt: What are the two key parameters you set when configuring LoRA for fine-tuning?

options.text: batch size and number of epochs

exercises.explain: When configuring LoRA parameters, you set the rank (r) and a scaling factor (alpha) to optimize model performance.

cards.title: What is SFT?
cards.body: SFT stands for Supervised Fine-Tuning. It’s the step where we adjust our model's weights using labeled data.

cards.title: Fine-Tuning Epochs
cards.body: An epoch is one complete cycle through the entire dataset. During fine-tuning, we run multiple epochs to help the model learn better.

cards.title: Learning Through Repetition
cards.body: Think of fine-tuning like studying for an exam. The more times you review your notes (epochs), the better you remember the material!

cards.title: Cross-Entropy Loss Example
cards.body: During training, we use cross-entropy loss to measure how well the model predicts labels. Lower loss means better performance!

cards.title: Myth: More Epochs Always Improve Performance
cards.body: It's a common myth that just running more epochs will always yield better results. Too many can lead to overfitting!

cards.title: Comparison of Loss Functions
cards.body: Let’s compare two loss functions used in training models: cross-entropy loss and mean squared error.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of using cross-entropy loss in fine-tuning?

options.text: To measure the difference between predicted and actual labels
To increase the model's accuracy directly
To decrease the number of epochs needed

exercises.explain: Cross-entropy loss measures how well the model's predictions match the actual labels, helping improve prediction accuracy.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Fine-tuning can continue indefinitely without negative effects.
exercises.explain: False. Continuing fine-tuning indefinitely can lead to overfitting, where the model performs poorly on new data.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: During supervised fine-tuning, we run multiple ________ to help the model learn from the dataset.
exercises.sentence: During supervised fine-tuning, we run multiple epochs to help the model learn from the dataset.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'epochs' since they represent complete passes through the data.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a correct statement about cross-entropy loss?

options.text: It is only used for regression tasks.
It helps in evaluating classification models.
It works the same as mean squared error.

exercises.explain: Cross-entropy loss is specifically designed for evaluating classification tasks, making it suitable for fine-tuning with discrete classes.

exercises.prompt: What is the key reason for using LoRA and cross-entropy loss during SFT?

options.text: To enable faster training and prevent overfitting.
To adjust model weights without overwhelming memory.
To lessen the need for labeled data during training.
To ensure the model learns from mistakes by measuring prediction errors.

exercises.explain: We use LoRA to adjust model weights in a memory-efficient way while using cross-entropy loss to measure prediction accuracy, allowing the model to improve from its errors.
